statements.text: If I have a terminal condition, I do not want cardiopulmonary resuscitation.
I direct that resuscitation not be initiated, including chest compressions, defibrillation, and artificial ventilation.
I would accept a breathing machine temporarily if my doctors expect me to recover, but I do not want to be kept on a ventilator indefinitely.
If I enroll in hospice, I want my implantable defibrillator turned off.
I do not want any heroic measures or to be kept alive by machines if I am dying.
I want to receive all treatments necessary to keep me alive, including CPR and a breathing machine.
If my kidneys fail and recovery is not expected, I do not want long-term dialysis.
Do not escalate medications to support my blood pressure if I would need to be transferred to the intensive care unit.
I do not want artificial nutrition or hydration through tubes if I am permanently unconscious.
I do not wish to receive antibiotics if I am in the final stage of dying.
I am willing to try BiPAP, but I never want a tube down my throat.
If my heart stops while I am in advanced heart failure with no reversible cause, do not attempt CPR.
